metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_2607
metadata.comment:
Rationale: From Results section:

"syntaxin 1A and nSec-1/Munc18-a were efficiently co-immunoprecipitated with anti-Vps11 antibody but not by normal rabbit IgG negative controls. This interaction is specific to the syntaxin 1A and nSec-1/Munc18-a, because other core components in the neuron specific membrane fusion, such as SNAP-25 and VAMP2, did not interact (Fig. 1A). To further confirm this interaction, we performed GST-pull down assays using rat brain homogenates. The endogenous mammalian Class C Vps protein complex was efficiently retained by GST-syntaxin 1A and GST-nSec-1/Munc18-a (Fig. 1B, lane 2 and lane 5), but not GST, GST-SNAP-25, or GST-VAMP2, respectively."

"Synaptosomal fractions were prepared from adult rat cortex [18,19]. The distribution profile of mammalian Class C Vps proteins was almost identical to those of syntaxin 1A, nSec-1/Munc18-a, and synaptophysin, which are found in fractions containing synaptic cytosolic and vesicular proteins"

"The Vps11-positive gold particles were observed in close proximity to the pre-synaptic plasma membrane (Fig. 3B, arrows). Using primary hippocampal neuronal cultures, mammalian Class C Vps proteins also co-localized with synapsin 1-positive processes and punctate synapses (Supplemental Fig. 2).". Experimental description: From M&M section:
"For immunoprecipitation, rat brain homogenates were lysed in modified RIPA buffer."
